hooks.text: The exact same currency has two prices on the same day. You pay the high one, and the business owner next door pays the low one.
Same currency. Same morning. Two different exchange rates. One door gives a worse one, and most business owners never find out which door they walked through.
Personal foreign exchange runs almost 2 percent worse than business rates. On a 20,000 dollar supplier payment, that gap quietly takes 300 dollars, with no line item anywhere.
A business owner pays a Chinese supplier 20,000 dollars every week. The rate looked fine. Nobody told him the same currency had a cheaper price one tier up.
If a business owner pays suppliers on personal foreign exchange rates, the worse price takes around 1,200 dollars a month. It never shows as a fee. It just disappears inside the rate.
